title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 7 (Fragment 1)
text:
The Magicians of Caprona

Chapter 7

Aunt Francesca’s challenge seemed to rally both sides. A female Petrocchi voice screamed, “We agree!” and all the muddy groups hastened towards the middle of the Corso again.

Paolo reached his family to hear Old Niccolo saying, “Don’t be a fool, Francesca!” He looked more like a muddy goblin than the head of a famous family. He was almost too breathless to speak.

“They have insulted us and fought us!” said Aunt Francesca. “They deserve to be disgraced and drummed out of Caprona. And I shall do it! I’m more than a match for a Petrocchi!” She looked it, vast and muddy as she was, with her huge black dress in tatters and her gray hair half undone and streaming over one shoulder.

But the other Montanas knew Aunt Francesca was an old woman. There was a chorus of protest. Uncle Lorenzo and Rinaldo both offered to take on the Petrocchi champion in her place.

“No,” said Old Niccolo. “Rinaldo, you were wounded—”

He was interrupted by catcalls from the Petrocchis. “Cowards! We want single combat!”

Old Niccolo’s muddy face screwed up with anger. “Very well, they shall have their single combat,” he said. “Antonio, I appoint you. Step forward.”

Paolo felt a gush of pride. So his father was, as he had always thought, the best spell-maker in the Casa Montana. But the pride became mixed with alarm, when Paolo saw the way his mother clutched Antonio’s arm, and the worried, reluctant look on his father’s mud-streaked face.

“Go on!” Old Niccolo said crossly.

Slowly, Antonio advanced into the space between the two families, stumbling a little among the loose cobbles. “I’m ready,” he called to the Petrocchis. “Who’s your champion?”

It was clear that there was some indecision among the Petrocchis. A dismayed voice said, “It’s Antonio!” This was followed by a babble of talk. From the turning of heads and the uncertain heaving about, Paolo thought they were looking for a Petrocchi who was unaccountably missing. But the fuss died away, and Guido Petrocchi himself stepped forward. Paolo could see several Petrocchis looking as alarmed as Elizabeth.

“I’m ready too,” said Guido, baring his teeth angrily. Since his face was plastered with mud, it made him look quite savage. He was also large and sturdy. He made Antonio look small, gentle and fragile. “And I demand an unlimited contest!” snarled Guido. He seemed even angrier than Old Niccolo.

“Very well,” Antonio said. There could have been the least shake in his voice. “You’re aware that means a fight to the finish, are you?”

“Suits me perfectly,” said Guido. He was like a giant saying “Feefi-fo-fum.” Paolo was suddenly very frightened.

It was at this moment that the Ducal Police arrived. They had come in, quietly and cunningly, riding bicycles along the pavements. No one noticed them until the Chief of Police and his lieutenant were standing beside the two champions.

“Guido Petrocchi and Antonio Montana,” said the lieutenant, “I arrest you—”

Both champions jumped, and turned to find blue braided uniforms on either side of them.

“Oh go away,” said Old Niccolo, hastening forward. “What do you have to interfere for?”

“Yes, go away,” said Guido. “We’re busy.”

The lieutenant flinched at Guido’s face, but the Chief of Police was a bold and dashing man with a handsome moustache, and he had his reputation to keep up as a bold and dashing man. He bowed to Old Niccolo. “These two are under arrest,” he said. “The rest of you I order to sink your differences and remember there is about to be a war.”

vocabulary.definition: To move forward.
vocabulary.example: Slowly, Antonio advanced into the space between the two families.

vocabulary.definition: A sudden fear or anxiety caused by awareness of danger.
vocabulary.example: The pride became mixed with alarm.

vocabulary.definition: To officially choose someone for a job or responsibility.
vocabulary.example: Antonio, I appoint you.

vocabulary.definition: Continuous, meaningless talk or chatter.
vocabulary.example: This was followed by a babble of talk.

vocabulary.definition: Showing courage and willingness to take risks.
vocabulary.example: A bold and dashing man with a handsome moustache.

vocabulary.definition: A whistle or shout of disapproval or derision, typically at a public event.
vocabulary.example: He was interrupted by catcalls from the Petrocchis.

vocabulary.definition: A call to take part in a contest or competition, especially a duel or fight.
vocabulary.example: Aunt Francesca’s challenge seemed to rally both sides.

vocabulary.definition: A person who fights or argues for a cause or on behalf of someone else; winner of a competition.
vocabulary.example: Uncle Lorenzo and Rinaldo both offered to take on the Petrocchi champion.

vocabulary.definition: A group of people speaking or shouting at the same time, or a simultaneous response.
vocabulary.example: There was a chorus of protest.

vocabulary.definition: To hold tightly or firmly.
vocabulary.example: Paolo saw the way his mother clutched Antonio’s arm.

vocabulary.definition: A fight or battle.
vocabulary.example: We want single combat!

vocabulary.definition: A person who lacks courage in facing danger or difficulty.
vocabulary.example: “Cowards! We want single combat!”

vocabulary.definition: In a clever and often deceitful way.
vocabulary.example: They arrived cunningly, riding bicycles along the pavements.

vocabulary.definition: Attractive, confident, and stylish in appearance or manner.
vocabulary.example: A bold and dashing man with a handsome moustache.

vocabulary.definition: Having lost respect or honour through a shameful action.
vocabulary.example: They deserve to be disgraced and drummed out of Caprona.

vocabulary.definition: Feeling distress or disappointment.
vocabulary.example: A dismayed voice said, “It’s Antonio!”

vocabulary.definition: A contest that continues until one side completely wins or surrenders.
vocabulary.example: You’re aware that means a fight to the finish, are you?

vocabulary.definition: A person acting unwisely or stupidly.
vocabulary.example: Don’t be a fool, Francesca!

vocabulary.definition: An imaginary, often mischievous or evil creature from folklore.
vocabulary.example: He looked more like a muddy goblin than the head of a famous family.

vocabulary.definition: Inability to make a decision quickly.
vocabulary.example: There was some indecision among the Petrocchis.

vocabulary.definition: To speak or act in a way that is offensive or disrespectful to somebody.
vocabulary.example: They have insulted us and fought us!

vocabulary.definition: To get involved in a situation where you are not wanted or needed.
vocabulary.example: What do you have to interfere for?

vocabulary.definition: A military or police rank below captain.
vocabulary.example: The Chief of Police and his lieutenant stood beside the champions.

vocabulary.definition: An opponent or competitor of equal ability.
vocabulary.example: I’m more than a match for a Petrocchi!

vocabulary.definition: Covered or saturated with mud.
vocabulary.example: He looked more like a muddy goblin than the head of a famous family.

vocabulary.definition: A feeling of pleasure or satisfaction about something you have done or a quality you have.
vocabulary.example: Paolo felt a gush of pride.

vocabulary.definition: To come together again and recover strength, or to bring people together to encourage support or fight.
vocabulary.example: Aunt Francesca’s challenge seemed to rally both sides.

vocabulary.definition: Not willing or enthusiastic about doing something.
vocabulary.example: The worried, reluctant look on his father’s mud-streaked face.

vocabulary.definition: The beliefs or opinions that are generally held about someone or something.
vocabulary.example: The Chief of Police had his reputation to keep up.

vocabulary.definition: To put aside disagreements or disputes.
vocabulary.example: I order you to sink your differences and remember there is about to be a war.

vocabulary.definition: To growl or speak in an angry or threatening way.
vocabulary.example: Guido snarled angrily.

vocabulary.definition: A person who casts magical spells.
vocabulary.example: His father was the best spell-maker in the Casa Montana.

vocabulary.definition: Flowing or hanging down in a continuous and steady way.
vocabulary.example: Her gray hair half undone and streaming over one shoulder.

vocabulary.definition: Strongly and solidly built.
vocabulary.example: He was also large and sturdy.

vocabulary.definition: A torn piece of cloth or clothing; rag.
vocabulary.example: Her huge black dress in tatters.

vocabulary.definition: A fight or competition without restrictions or time limits, often to the finish.
vocabulary.example: I demand an unlimited contest!

vocabulary.definition: Of very great extent or size; immense.
vocabulary.example: She looked vast and muddy as she was, with her huge black dress in tatters.

vocabulary.definition: To injure a person, especially by making a hole in the skin with a weapon.
vocabulary.example: Rinaldo, you were wounded—
